summary: Charles Leclerc outran George Russell through a dramatic 56-lap battle to secure victory at Silverstone, capitalizing on a late-race opportunity after Russell led for much of the afternoon. The result proved costly for Mercedes, with both Kimi Antonelli—who had claimed pole position and won Saturday's sprint—and title rival Max Verstappen finishing outside the points. Leclerc's win narrows the championship gap as Ferrari strengthens its challenge, while Mercedes' difficult afternoon threatens to derail its momentum in the constructors' battle.
